text: The surgeon demonstrates the technique for creating a laparoscopic slip knot. The suture is loaded, and a bite is taken. The first throw is made, leaving the knot loose like an air knot.

The surgeon performs the next throw of the laparoscopic slip knot, tightening it horizontally into an air knot. The needle end of the suture is brought towards the five o'clock position on the screen to create tension. The opposite leg of the suture is pulled towards the Penrose drain, causing the knot to visibly flip.

The surgeon maintains tension with the right hand, sliding along the suture to push the knot down while pulling with the right hand, forming a snug slip knot. The body and tail of the suture are then tightened horizontally to square the knot.
Now that should actually hold your tension very nicely so that you can place additional throws. without losing your tension note. It's a very useful technique for closing the diaphragmatic cura.
